title: daphne zephyrina
intro: Daphne zephyrina, also known as the fragrant daphne, is a flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a shrub native to the Himalayan regions and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Indian medicine. The plant contains daphnin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Daphne zephyrina are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
